actions.label: Build Delivery Foundation
actions.description: Choose your approach: Do the Work Yourself (you = the business, you learn everything) or Hire First Contractor (offload some delivery, reclaim your time).
actions.narrative_success: Everything got done. Either you did it all personally and learned the business cold, or your contractor handled 80% so you could focus on growth.
actions.narrative_failure: You delivered, but one thing slipped. A good reminder that one person can't do everything.

actions.label: Document Your Process
actions.description: Choose what to document: SOPs (how you actually do the work), Quality Control (checklist so work goes out right), or Client Onboarding (smooth intake and first-week experience).
actions.narrative_success: For the first time, your process exists outside your head. SOPs are written, QC checklists catch errors, onboarding is smooth. You look like a real company.
actions.narrative_failure: The documentation is incomplete — you realized how much of your process is instinct. But you've started the work.

actions.label: Automate the Small Stuff
actions.description: Email templates, invoice automation, scheduling tools. Reclaim hours from admin busywork.
actions.narrative_success: Invoices go out automatically. Scheduling is self-serve. You just got back five hours a week you didn't know you were losing.
actions.narrative_failure: Partially automated. Invoices work perfectly; scheduling still needs tweaking. But you're getting hours back.

actions.label: Scale Your Delivery
actions.description: Choose your approach: Build a Delivery Team (2-3 people on fulfillment) or Build Fulfillment System (end-to-end intake→delivery→follow-up automation).
actions.narrative_success: The team handled this month's workload without escalation to you. Or the system is so smooth that a new client moves through like a product on an assembly line.
actions.narrative_failure: The infrastructure is built. Quality isn't where you want it yet. You're in the management-heavy phase. Month two improves this.

actions.label: Manage the Team
actions.description: Choose your tool: Implement Project Management (Asana/Monday/Basecamp for visibility) or Build Hiring Pipeline (repeatable process for finding and onboarding team members).
actions.narrative_success: For the first time, you can see every project, deadline, and bottleneck in one screen. Or your hiring pipeline means next hire takes days, not weeks.
actions.narrative_failure: The tool is set up. Adoption is mixed. People problems, not software problems — but the structure is in place.

actions.label: Hire Specialized Roles
actions.description: Choose your hire: Content Creator (brand grows on autopilot) or Client Success Manager (retention, you're not the only person clients call).
actions.narrative_success: The content creator's posts are getting engagement. The CSM's follow-ups are preventing churn. Either way, you bought back yourself.
actions.narrative_failure: The hire needs training but the direction is right. Give them one more month.

actions.label: Promote a Manager
actions.description: Hire or promote someone to manage the team. You manage one person instead of five. The team grows without constant your oversight.
actions.narrative_success: She ran Monday's meeting without you. The team didn't even notice you weren't there. This is what delegation feels like.
actions.narrative_failure: The new manager is learning. Team dynamics shifted. Growing pains, but you see the light at the end of the tunnel.

actions.label: Own Your Time
actions.description: Full systemization: the business runs without you. SOPs for everything, manager handles daily ops. You're the owner, not the operator.
actions.narrative_success: You took a week off. Revenue was the same when you came back. Emails were handled. Clients were fine. This is what freedom feels like.
actions.narrative_failure: Almost there. Two client issues escalated to you during your vacation. Close, but the safety net has holes. Month two fixes this.

actions.label: Build Competitive Moat
actions.description: Choose your approach: Proprietary IP (tools/methodology competitors can't replicate) or Vertical Integration (bring supplier/distribution in-house, keep more margin).
actions.narrative_success: The proprietary framework is built and branded. Clients come to you for the methodology now. Or you own the supply chain — margins jumped.
actions.narrative_failure: The moat is developed but not yet differentiated. More iteration needed. Still, the foundation is strong.

actions.label: Expand to New Market
actions.description: Clone what works into a new geography or vertical. Open a second location or vertical. Scale your empire.
actions.narrative_success: Location two is profitable by month three. The playbook translated. You're an empire now.
actions.narrative_failure: The new market is slower than expected. Overhead is real. Six months to break even if things go right.
